claims.claim: code_ref agent_activity.py:2086 anchors the pre-tool filler-speech mechanism
claims.found: Line 2086: `if preemptive := self._preemptive_generation:` — this is the PREEMPTIVE GENERATION path (starting LLM gen during user speech), not filler/pre-tool acknowledgement. grep for filler|pre-tool|acknowled|let me check across voice/ returns nothing. No filler mechanism exists here.
claims.correction: remove the code_ref (or move to LAT-09). livekit-agents has no pre-tool-filler implementation; the note's own claim is sourced entirely from el-orchestration/together-talk, not this file. Line 2086 belongs to preemptive generation, which the note itself says is 'orthogonal' (LAT-09).

claims.claim: ElevenLabs Immediate Mode combines pre-tool speech; agent emits brief acknowledgement 'Let me check that for you' while tool runs in parallel, minimizing dead air
claims.found: line 29: 'Immediate Mode... Combined with pre-tool speech, agent first emits a brief acknowledgement ("Let me check that for you") returned to the user while the tool runs in parallel, minimizing dead air.'

claims.claim: For slower tools ElevenLabs automatically extends filler messages to match the expected wait time
claims.found: line 29: 'For slower tools the platform automatically extends these filler messages to match the expected wait time.'

claims.claim: Post-tool speech mode delays execution for consequential actions (transfer, payment); user hears full context and can interrupt before action
claims.found: line 30: 'Post-Tool Speech Mode — delays execution until the agent has finished speaking. For consequential actions (transfer, end session, submit payment). User hears full context and can interrupt before action.'

claims.claim: Together thinker-talker: small fast LLM handles live conversation, emits filler ('let me think about it') + issues one tool call to a much bigger model whose response goes to TTS
claims.found: summary line 43 + raw transcript: 'thinker talker pattern... small LLM... produces a response... let me think about it... issues one big tool one tool call to a much bigger model that then has... better instructions, has all the tools.'

claims.claim: Guardrails sit before TTS because 'you can't take back spoken words'
claims.found: summary line 42: 'guardrails after LLM generation, before TTS — because "you can't take back spoken words".' Raw transcript: 'You can't take back things that are spoken.' line 53: 'Guardrails MUST sit before TTS (irreversible speech).'

claims.claim: Filler is a perceived-latency technique: v2v clock to first-audio is met by the acknowledgement even though substantive answer arrives later
claims.cited: (synthesis from el-orchestration 'minimizing dead air' + together filler)
claims.found: Logical synthesis consistent with both sources: pre-tool/filler speech fills airtime ('minimizing dead air') while the slow tool/big model runs in parallel. No source contradicts.

claims.claim: [[LAT-05-ttft-target-model-size]] — big model 'would otherwise burn the budget' link
claims.found: Cross-note link cited as supporting rationale; not opened. Note does not assert LAT-05 'proves' a specific value, only contextual cross-reference.

summary: All six source-figure/mechanism claims (ElevenLabs pre-tool/post-tool speech, Together thinker-talker, guardrail-before-TTS) are fully SUPPORTED against el-orchestration and the together-talk summary + raw youtube transcript ground truth. The single code_ref (agent_activity.py:2086) is WRONG: line 2086 is the preemptive-generation branch, not any filler/pre-tool mechanism, and no filler implementation exists in livekit-agents voice/. The note's prose even labels preemptive generation 'orthogonal' (LAT-09), so the code anchor is misapplied.
